title: Integrated B.Sc. and M.Sc. Forensic Science
content: The Integrated B.Sc. + M.Sc. in Forensic Science is a comprehensive five-year program designed to equip students with a deep understanding of forensic principles through a seamless blend of undergraduate and postgraduate education. Emphasizing a hands-on learning experience, the program incorporates extensive laboratory training, fieldwork, and real-time case study analyses in state-of-the-art forensic labs. Students gain practical skills in areas such as crime scene investigation, toxicology, DNA analysis, digital forensics, and forensic pathology. Our modern infrastructure, experienced faculty, and industry collaborations ensure a dynamic and application-oriented curriculum aligned with current forensic trends. Graduates are well-prepared for careers in law enforcement agencies, forensic laboratories, intelligence bureaus, legal consultancies, cybercrime cells, and research organizations. The program also provides a strong foundation for doctoral studies and specialized research in forensic science. This integrated approach opens a wide spectrum of professional and academic opportunities in the rapidly growing and evolving field of forensic science. XIIth with Science with minimum 50% marks Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 01 Comprehensive Forensic Expertise: Provide integrated knowledge of core forensic disciplines (biology, chemistry, digital forensics, crime scene investigation) and advanced analytical techniques. 02 Practical and Technical Proficiency: Develop hands-on skills in evidence collection, lab analysis, and modern forensic technologies (e.g., DNA sequencing, toxicology, cyber forensics). 03 Legal and Ethical Foundation : Train students to navigate judicial systems, uphold ethical standards, and effectively communicate forensic findings in legal contexts. 04 Research and Innovation: Foster critical thinking and research capabilities to address emerging challenges in forensics through projects, dissertations, and interdisciplinary collaboration. 05 Professional Readiness : Prepare graduates for careers in forensic labs, law enforcement, or academia via internships, mock crime scenarios, and industry-aligned training. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 02 03 01 02 03
